title: Introduction
content:
## Introduction

The Core Information Standard Version 2 (CIS V2) defines the essential data elements required for effective health and care information exchange across the UK. This standard ensures consistent capture and sharing of person-centred information to support integrated care delivery.

### Purpose and Scope

This Digital Adaptation Kit (DAK) provides implementation guidance for the Core Information Standard V2. It covers the fundamental information requirements for person demographics, care preferences, clinical data, and care coordination across health and social care settings. The standard applies to all digital systems that need to exchange person-centred information within the UK health and care ecosystem.

### Background

Developed through extensive stakeholder consultation, CIS V2 builds upon previous standards to provide a comprehensive framework for information sharing. It addresses the need for consistent, structured data capture while maintaining flexibility for local requirements and person-centred care approaches.

title: Health Interventions & Recommendations
content:
## Health Interventions & Recommendations

### Key Recommendations

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Person Demographics | Capture mandatory elements: name, date of birth, NHS number, address | NHS Data Dictionary | A |
| Individual Requirements | Record all reasonable adjustments per Equality Act 2010 | Equality Act 2010 | A |
| Safeguarding | Document safeguarding concerns with appropriate coding | SNOMED CT Reference Sets | A |
| Consent Recording | Obtain explicit consent for information sharing | UK GDPR/DPA 2018 | A |
| Care Planning | Create person-centred care plans with goals and actions | NICE Guidelines | B |
| Risk Assessment | Document risks to self/others with mitigation strategies | Clinical Best Practice | B |
| Medication Management | Use dm+d for all medication coding | NHS Digital | A |
| End of Life Care | Record CPR decisions and preferred place of death | ReSPECT Process | A |

These recommendations ensure comprehensive, person-centred information capture while maintaining compliance with UK regulations and clinical best practices.

title: Generic Personas
content:
## Generic Personas

### System User Personas

| Persona ID | Title | Description | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | GP Practice Administrator | Manages patient registration and demographics | Data entry, PDS integration, consent management |
| CISV2.P2 | Hospital Clinician | Reviews and updates clinical information during encounters | Clinical assessment, risk evaluation, care planning |
| CISV2.P3 | Community Nurse | Provides care in community settings, updates care records | Home visits, medication administration, care coordination |
| CISV2.P4 | Social Worker | Manages safeguarding and social care assessments | Safeguarding procedures, mental capacity assessment, care packages |
| CISV2.P5 | Care Coordinator | Oversees integrated care delivery across settings | Multi-agency coordination, care plan reviews, service integration |
| CISV2.P6 | Person/Carer | Individual receiving care or their representative | Self-advocacy, care preferences, consent provision |

Each persona interacts with different aspects of the Core Information Standard based on their role and responsibilities within the care system.

title: User Scenarios
content:
## User Scenarios

### Scenario 1: New Patient Registration

A person moves to a new area and registers with a GP practice. The practice administrator needs to capture comprehensive demographic information and obtain consent for information sharing.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Actors | GP Practice Administrator (CISV2.P1), Person (CISV2.P6) |
| Data Elements | Person demographics, NHS number, contact details, consent |
| Process | Registration workflow (CISV2.BP1) |
| Outcome | Complete patient record established with appropriate consents |

### Scenario 2: Hospital Admission with Complex Needs

An elderly person with multiple conditions is admitted to hospital. The clinical team needs to access their care preferences, medication history, and any advance decisions.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Actors | Hospital Clinician (CISV2.P2), Person/Carer (CISV2.P6) |
| Data Elements | Problem list, medications, individual requirements, legal information |
| Process | Admission assessment workflow (CISV2.BP2) |
| Outcome | Comprehensive admission assessment with person-centred care plan |

### Scenario 3: Safeguarding Concern

A community nurse identifies potential safeguarding issues during a home visit. They need to document concerns and initiate appropriate referrals.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Actors | Community Nurse (CISV2.P3), Social Worker (CISV2.P4) |
| Data Elements | Safeguarding indicators, risk assessment, professional contacts |
| Process | Safeguarding referral workflow (CISV2.BP3) |
| Outcome | Safeguarding concern documented and referred appropriately |

title: Business Processes & Workflows
content: Core workflows include patient registration, clinical assessments, care planning, and information sharing processes.

processes_data.summary_markdown: Core workflows include patient registration, clinical assessments, care planning, and information sharing processes.

activities.title: Patient Registration Workflow
activities.objectives: Capture complete demographic information and establish consent for information sharing

activities.title: Clinical Assessment Workflow
activities.objectives: Perform comprehensive clinical assessment including individual requirements and care preferences

activities.title: Information Sharing Workflow
activities.objectives: Share person information across care settings with appropriate consent and security

title: Core Data Elements
content:
## Core Data Elements

### Person Demographics Domain

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE1 | Person First Name | First name(s) including middle names | String[35] | M | NHS DD: Person given name |
| CISV2.DE2 | Person Family Name | Family name or surname | String[35] | M | NHS DD: Person family name |
| CISV2.DE3 | Date of Birth | Person's date of birth | Date | M | NHS DD: Person birth date |
| CISV2.DE4 | NHS Number | Unique NHS identifier | String[10] | R | NHS DD: NHS number |
| CISV2.DE5 | Address Line 1 | First line of address | String[35] | M | NHS DD: Patient usual address |
| CISV2.DE6 | Postcode | Postal code | String[8] | R | NHS DD: Postcode of usual address |

### Individual Requirements Domain

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE7 | Reasonable Adjustment Flag | Requires adjustments per Equality Act | Boolean | R | SNOMED: 1108111000000107 |
| CISV2.DE8 | Communication Method | Preferred communication method | String[1] | R | NHS DD: Communication contact method |
| CISV2.DE9 | Impairment Category | Type of impairment requiring adjustment | Code | R | SNOMED CT reference set |

### Clinical Information Domain

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE10 | Problem | Clinical problem or diagnosis | Code/Text | M | SNOMED: Health issues reference set |
| CISV2.DE11 | Medication Name | Name of medication | Code/Text | M | dm+d: VTM/VMP/AMP |
| CISV2.DE12 | Allergy Causative Agent | Substance causing allergy | Code/Text | M | SNOMED/dm+d |
| CISV2.DE13 | Procedure | Clinical procedure performed | Code/Text | M | SNOMED: 71388002 |

title: Decision-Support Logic
content:
## Decision-Support Logic

### Table 1: Consent for Information Sharing

**Description:** Determines appropriate information sharing based on consent status and capacity

| Consent Status | Mental Capacity | Legitimate Interest | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Explicit consent given | Has capacity | N/A | Share full record |
| No consent given | Has capacity | No | Do not share |
| No consent given | Has capacity | Yes (emergency) | Share minimum necessary |
| Unable to consent | Lacks capacity | Best interests decision documented | Share per best interests |
| Consent withdrawn | Has capacity | No | Stop sharing immediately |

### Table 2: Reasonable Adjustment Requirements

**Description:** Determines required adjustments based on impairment type

| Impairment Type | Communication Need | Environmental Need | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Visual impairment | Large print/audio | Good lighting, clear signage | Provide accessible formats |
| Hearing impairment | BSL interpreter | Hearing loop | Book interpreter, ensure equipment |
| Learning disability | Easy read | Quiet space, extra time | Prepare easy read materials |
| Autism | Clear, direct communication | Minimal sensory stimulation | Adjust environment |
| Physical disability | Standard | Wheelchair access | Ensure accessible facilities |

### Table 3: Safeguarding Escalation

**Description:** Determines safeguarding response based on risk indicators

| Risk Type | Severity | Person Consent | Output Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Self-harm risk | High | Refuses support | Immediate mental health referral |
| Neglect indicators | Moderate | Agrees to support | Social services assessment |
| Financial abuse | Any level | N/A | Adult safeguarding referral |
| Child at risk | Any level | N/A | Immediate child protection referral |

title: Indicators & Performance Metrics
content:
## Indicators & Performance Metrics

### Quality Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.I1 | Demographics Completeness | Records with all mandatory demographics | All active records | By organisation | Monthly |
| CISV2.I2 | Consent Documentation Rate | Records with valid consent | All records requiring consent | By consent type | Monthly |
| CISV2.I3 | Reasonable Adjustment Recording | Persons with adjustments documented | Persons flagged as requiring adjustments | By adjustment type | Quarterly |
| CISV2.I4 | Care Plan Currency | Care plans reviewed within timeframe | All active care plans | By care setting | Monthly |
| CISV2.I5 | Risk Assessment Completion | Completed risk assessments | Admissions requiring assessment | By risk type | Weekly |

### Process Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.I6 | PDS Match Rate | Successful PDS matches | All PDS queries | By source system | Daily |
| CISV2.I7 | Information Sharing Timeliness | Shares completed within SLA | All sharing requests | By urgency | Daily |
| CISV2.I8 | Medication Reconciliation | Reconciled medication lists | Hospital admissions | By ward | Daily |

title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
content:
## Functional & Non-Functional Requirements

### Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.F1 | PDS Integration | System must integrate with Personal Demographics Service for NHS number verification | High |
| CISV2.F2 | Consent Management | Capture, store and enforce consent decisions for information sharing | High |
| CISV2.F3 | Clinical Coding | Support SNOMED CT, dm+d, LOINC coding with text fallback | High |
| CISV2.F4 | Audit Trail | Maintain complete audit trail of all data access and modifications | High |
| CISV2.F5 | Care Plan Versioning | Support versioned care plans with change tracking | Medium |
| CISV2.F6 | Document Management | Store and retrieve clinical documents with appropriate metadata | Medium |
| CISV2.F7 | Workflow Support | Implement defined business processes with appropriate routing | Medium |
| CISV2.F8 | Report Generation | Generate standard reports for quality indicators | Low |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.N1 | Performance | Response time <2 seconds for record retrieval | High |
| CISV2.N2 | Availability | 99.9% availability during core hours (8am-8pm) | High |
| CISV2.N3 | Security | Implement NHS Data Security Standards | High |
| CISV2.N4 | Accessibility | WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for all user interfaces | High |
| CISV2.N5 | Scalability | Support 100,000+ patient records per instance | Medium |
| CISV2.N6 | Interoperability | FHIR R4 UK Core compliant interfaces | High |
| CISV2.N7 | Data Retention | Comply with NHS Records Management Code | High |
| CISV2.N8 | Business Continuity | RPO <1 hour, RTO <4 hours | Medium |

title: References
content:
## References

### Standards and Specifications

1. NHS Data Dictionary. (2023). *NHS Data Model and Dictionary Service*. NHS Digital. https://www.datadictionary.nhs.uk/

2. NHS Digital. (2023). *Dictionary of medicines and devices (dm+d)*. https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/pharmacies-gp-practices-and-appliance-contractors/dictionary-medicines-and-devices-dmd

3. SNOMED International. (2023). *SNOMED CT UK Edition*. https://termbrowser.nhs.uk/

4. HL7 UK. (2023). *UK Core FHIR Implementation Guide*. https://simplifier.net/hl7fhirukcorer4

### Legislation and Policy

5. UK Government. (2010). *Equality Act 2010*. https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2010/15/contents

6. UK Government. (2018). *Data Protection Act 2018*. https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2018/12/contents

7. NHS England. (2022). *Records Management Code of Practice 2021*. https://www.nhsx.nhs.uk/information-governance/guidance/records-management-code/

### Clinical Guidelines

8. NICE. (2023). *Patient experience in adult NHS services: improving the experience of care for people using adult NHS services*. Clinical guideline [CG138].

9. Resuscitation Council UK. (2023). *ReSPECT (Recommended Summary Plan for Emergency Care and Treatment)*. https://www.resus.org.uk/respect

10. Royal College of Physicians. (2023). *National Early Warning Score (NEWS) 2*. https://www.rcplondon.ac.uk/projects/outputs/national-early-warning-score-news-2

### Technical Resources

11. PRSB. (2023). *Core Information Standard v2.0*. Professional Record Standards Body. https://theprsb.org/standards/

12. NHS Digital. (2023). *Spine Core Services*. https://digital.nhs.uk/services/spine
